metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC2 to specify the assertion target for `show` output (e.g. 'assert via `rebar show <id> --json | jq -e .name,.email,.mappings,.keys` that all four fields are present and non-empty') so the proving command is unambiguous.
findings.finding: AC2 states 'asserts all four fields render' without defining what 'render' means — the criterion cannot be evaluated without knowing whether the assertion is on structured output (JSON), prose stdout text, or some other artefact, making the test specification ambiguous and the AC not fully measurable.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'asserts all four fields render' with 'asserts via structured output (e.g. `--json | jq -e`) that name, email, mappings, and keys are present and match the values supplied at create time'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `transition_close.py` and `gates.py` to the registration site list (and to `file_impact`) — both contain independent hardcoded exempt-type tuples (`session_log`, `code_review`) that are NOT derived from `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` and must be explicitly patched to include `identity`.
findings.finding: Two hardcoded exempt-type tuples in `transition_close.py` and `gates.py` are not in the plan's 7-site registration list; both check session_log/code_review independently (not derived from _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES), so `identity` will be silently skipped by these guards without explicit additions.
findings.location: Section 1 ('Register identity as an exempt ticket-type') / file_impact list — `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:99` and `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py:118`
findings.suggested_fix:
Extend Section 1's site list with:
- `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:99` — add `"identity"` to `if ticket_type in ("session_log", "code_review")` so the LLM close-verifier is skipped before transition_core refuses.
- `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py:118` — add `"identity"` to `if ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review")` in `plan_review_precheck`.
Also add both files to `file_impact`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` (line 118, `plan_review_gate` exempt tuple) and `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (line 99, `_completion_precheck` exempt tuple) to the file_impact list and Section 1 site enumeration, since both contain hardcoded `("session_log", "code_review")` tuples that do NOT delegate to `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES`.
findings.finding: The plan's 'EVERY site' claim misses two hardcoded exemption tuples — `gates.py:118` (`('bug', 'session_log', 'code_review')`, the plan-review-for-claim gate) and `transition_close.py:99` (`('session_log', 'code_review')`, the completion-verification close gate) — that do NOT delegate to `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` and thus will not be patched by the seven named changes. Updating only the seven listed sites leaves `identity` tickets potentially blocked by these two lifecycle gates.
findings.location: Section 1 ('Register identity as an exempt ticket-type') / file_impact list — `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py:118` and `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:99` are absent
findings.suggested_fix: Add to file_impact: `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` (add `"identity"` to the tuple at line 118) and `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (add `"identity"` to the tuple at line 99). These follow the exact same exemption logic as the named seven sites.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py` to the edit list and update `_GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES` (line 26) to include `"identity"` so the per-ticket gates (check_ac, clarity_check, quality_check) treat identity tickets as exempt.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_engine_support/gates.py` contains `_GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES = ("session_log", "code_review")` (line 26), used by all three per-ticket quality gates (check_ac, clarity_check, quality_check). It is not named in the plan's seven-site list or `file_impact`, so `identity` tickets will fail these gates rather than passing trivially.
findings.suggested_fix: _GATE_EXEMPT_TYPES = ("session_log", "code_review", "identity")

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` to the edit list and update the inline tuple at line 118 to include `"identity"` so the plan-review start-work gate is also exempt for identity tickets.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` has an inline exemption tuple `("bug", "session_log", "code_review")` at line 118 inside `claim_gate_check()`, the plan-review START-WORK gate called by both `claim` and `transition open -> in_progress`. This file is absent from the plan's seven-site list and `file_impact`.
findings.suggested_fix: if ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review", "identity"):

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` (line 99) and `src/rebar/_commands/gates.py` (line 118) to the seven-site exemption list and to `file_impact`, patching their hardcoded inline tuples to include `'identity'`.
findings.finding: The plan's seven-site exemption list omits two files with hardcoded inline lifecycle-exempt type tuples that will NOT automatically pick up 'identity' when _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES is updated: `transition_close.py:99` (`if ticket_type in ('session_log', 'code_review')`) and `gates.py:118` (`if ticket_type in ('bug', 'session_log', 'code_review')`). Both are standalone inline tuples, not references to _NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES, so patching only the seven listed sites leaves identity tickets raisable by the close-gate guard and plan-review claim guard.
findings.location: §1 'Register identity as an exempt ticket-type' / file_impact list; src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py:99; src/rebar/_commands/gates.py:118
findings.suggested_fix: In transition_close.py line 99, change `if ticket_type in ("session_log", "code_review")` to `if ticket_type in ("session_log", "code_review", "identity")`. In gates.py line 118, change `if ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review")` to `if ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review", "identity")`. Add both files to the plan's file_impact list and §1 site enumeration.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` to the edit list and update the inline tuple at line 99 to include `"identity"` so the completion-verification pre-check exempts identity tickets without firing an LLM call.
findings.finding: `src/rebar/_commands/transition_close.py` has an inline exemption tuple `("session_log", "code_review")` at line 99, inside the completion-verification close gate pre-check. It is not named in the plan's seven-site list or `file_impact`.
findings.suggested_fix: if ticket_type in ("session_log", "code_review", "identity"):

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify whether `keys[].algorithm` stores the SSH key-type name (e.g. `ssh-ed25519`, already present in the `public_key` string) or the attest scheme name (e.g. `sshsig`), and reconcile the stated reference to 'the foundation attest scheme vocabulary' with the actual attest registry's naming convention.
findings.finding: The plan's `keys[].algorithm` field is described as matching 'the foundation attest scheme vocabulary (e.g. ssh-ed25519)', but the foundation attest substrate (`rebar/attest/sshsig.py`) uses scheme names ('sshsig') — not key-type names ('ssh-ed25519'). The key-type string 'ssh-ed25519' is already embedded inside the `public_key` authorized-keys line itself, making a separate `algorithm` field that duplicates it potentially redundant or inconsistent with the attest registry's naming.
findings.location: What > Section 2, `keys` field definition: '`algorithm` matches the foundation attest scheme vocabulary (e.g. `ssh-ed25519`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'algorithm matches the foundation attest scheme vocabulary (e.g. ssh-ed25519)' with an explicit definition: `algorithm` is the SSH key-type string as it appears in the authorized-keys line (e.g. `ssh-ed25519`, `ecdsa-sha2-nistp256`). Note it is distinct from the attest scheme name (`sshsig`), which covers all SSH key types regardless of algorithm. This avoids confusion with `rebar/attest/registry.py`'s scheme vocabulary.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify who writes `.rebar/current_identity` and when (e.g., `rebar identity create --set` auto-writes it, or a separate `rebar identity use <id>` command writes it), and add that write path to `file_impact` and AC criterion (c).
findings.finding: The write/create path for `.rebar/current_identity` is never specified: the plan describes reading and resolving it in detail but never says which command (or user action) creates or updates the file, leaving the state lifecycle incomplete.
findings.location: Section 3 — Self-identity config pointer; AC criterion (c)
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `rebar identity use <id>` command (or a `--set` flag on create) that atomically writes `{"identity_id": "<id>"}` to `.rebar/current_identity`, and add a bullet to AC (c) asserting a round-trip: create → use → resolve returns the created id.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the CLI serialisation format for `--mapping` (e.g. `--mapping provider=jira,external_id=USERID` or `--mapping '{"provider":"jira","external_id":"USERID"}'`) and the corresponding `--key` format so an executor can wire CLI/lib/MCP without ambiguity.
findings.finding: The CLI argument format for the `mappings` field is unspecified: an executor cannot determine whether to use `--mapping jira:userid123`, `--mapping provider=jira,external_id=userid123`, `--mapping '{"provider":"jira","external_id":"..."}'`, or repeated flag pairs without stopping to choose or ask.
findings.location: Section 2 (Create path) / file_impact: new identity module
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Section 2: 'CLI accepts `--mapping PROVIDER:EXTERNAL_ID` (colon-separated, flag repeatable) and `--key PUBLIC_KEY_LINE` (flag repeatable, value is the full OpenSSH authorized-keys line). Library and MCP accept the structured list directly.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit fallback for subprocess failure (OSError, CalledProcessError, timeout) in `resolve_current_identity()` so any git invocation error is treated as 'email unavailable → None', consistent with the plan's opt-in guarantee.
findings.finding: The resolver calls `git config user.email` (a subprocess) but only handles the logical 'unset/unconfigured' case; it does not specify behavior when the subprocess itself fails (git not installed, OSError, non-zero exit code, timeout), so those errors may propagate rather than degrade gracefully.
findings.suggested_fix: Wrap the `git config user.email` call in a try/except (OSError, subprocess.CalledProcessError) with an optional log-at-DEBUG, returning None on any error; add a test case asserting the resolver returns None when git is absent.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `orchestrator.py:563` (`drift_refresh`'s type guard `if ctx.ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review")`) to the seven-site exemption list as an eighth distinct patch site.
findings.finding: The plan enumerates `orchestrator.py` as one gate-exempt site, but `orchestrator.py` contains TWO separate exempt-type tuple literals that both need updating: the one in `_exempt_verdict`'s caller path (workflow_ops.py:79, which the plan correctly names) and the one inside `drift_refresh` at orchestrator.py:563 (`if ctx.ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review"): return None`). The `drift_refresh` guard is a separate code site that the plan's seven-site enumeration does not call out — omitting it would leave `identity` tickets eligible for drift-refresh despite being non-dispatchable artifacts.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"identity"` to the tuple at `orchestrator.py:563`: `if ctx.ticket_type in ("bug", "session_log", "code_review", "identity"): return None`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly name `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` in `composer.py` (line 41) as a distinct patch site in the seven-site list (or add it as an eighth), confirming `identity` is added to it alongside `session_log`.
findings.finding: The plan's seven-site enumeration omits `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` in `composer.py` (line 41: `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES = ("bug", "session_log", "code_review")`). The plan vaguely says 'confirm the file-impact-exempt / type lists treat it like session_log' inside the `composer.py` bullet, but does not name `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` as a discrete constant, and the constant is absent from the `file_impact` section. If omitted, a freshly-created `identity` ticket will emit a spurious 'Warning: no file_impact recorded' nudge at create-time, even though `identity` tickets never carry `file_impact`.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `"identity"` to `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES` at `composer.py:41`: `_FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES = ("bug", "session_log", "code_review", "identity")`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly address export behavior for identity tickets: specify whether they are hidden by default (and thus covered by `--include-session-logs`), or require a separate `--include-identity` flag, or are always included — and add this to file_impact if `export_ndjson.py` / `_io/_cli.py` need changes.
findings.finding: Adding `'identity'` to `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` causes `export_ndjson.py` to hide identity tickets unless the caller passes `--include-session-logs` (flag name and boolean variable are both named `include_session_logs`, making identity tickets subordinate to a session-log-named flag). The plan does not acknowledge this semantic side-effect or specify whether identity tickets should be included in default exports, opt-in via the existing session-log flag, or require a new flag.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the `resolve_current_identity()` resolution cascade (pointer hit → git-email match → None, WARNING on ambiguity, dangling-pointer fallthrough) to an ADR or CLAUDE.md entry — either extend 4b5b's scope explicitly or add a new doc file to this ticket's file_impact.
findings.finding: The `resolve_current_identity()` resolution cascade (pointer → git-email case-insensitive match → None, with WARNING on ambiguity and specific dangling-pointer fallthrough semantics) is a new foundational contract that downstream epic tickets (4e9d, bde5, etc.) will depend on, but neither this ticket's file_impact nor the referenced sibling ADR (4b5b) is confirmed to document this logic. The plan only says 4b5b records 'provider-vocabulary and key-format decisions' — the resolver contract is distinct and may be undocumented.
findings.suggested_fix: Either (a) expand 4b5b's ADR to cover the resolution cascade and reference it explicitly from this ticket's file_impact, or (b) add a new `docs/adr/identity-resolution.md` (or CLAUDE.md section) to this ticket's file_impact that records the cascade, the WARNING-not-error decision on ambiguity, and the fallthrough on dangling pointer.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the validation contract for `rebar identity create` inputs: minimally, email format check, public-key line format check (starts with algorithm token matching `algorithm` field), and duplicate-mapping rejection, with specified CLI error messages and non-zero exit codes.
findings.finding: The `rebar identity create` command specifies the data fields but provides no input validation contract for invalid values (malformed email, unrecognised/malformed public-key line, duplicate provider+external_id pair), leaving the non-happy-path UX undefined.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a forward-compat note to the `keys` schema (or the Scope section) stating how ticket e165 (key rotation) will extend the CREATE-only schema — e.g. by specifying that UPDATE events appending/revoking individual key entries are the reserved extension point, so implementers of e165 do not need to introduce a breaking schema change.
findings.finding: The plan adopts a single-CREATE, flat `keys` list for identity records without acknowledging the known forward-compatibility risk for key rotation. The project's own prior-art analysis (docs/remediation-implementation-plan.md) explicitly warns that 'rebar's flat in-event identity cannot express rotation' and that 'git-bug needed a dedicated migration tool to add this later' — yet the plan's CREATE schema (one event, one flat keys list) repeats exactly that footgun without documenting what forward-compat story (e.g. UPDATE events adding/removing keys, versioned identity DAG) is reserved for ticket e165.
findings.location: What > Section 2 (`keys` field); Scope ('key rotation (e165) [out of scope]')

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the post-rollback schema state: specify whether rolling back after identity tickets exist requires a compensating-forward step (e.g., re-adding the enum value, or migrating existing identity tickets to a tombstone type), and note DATA LOSS RISK if the back-out path is destructive.
findings.finding: The plan does not address the post-rollback schema state after identity tickets have been created: rolling back the 'identity' enum addition while identity-typed events remain in the store would leave those events referencing an unrecognised ticket_type, potentially causing reducer/validation failures on read.
findings.location: ## What / Section 1 (register identity at all seven sites); ## Acceptance Criteria AC-1; file_impact: src/rebar/schemas/common.schema.json
findings.suggested_fix: Add a rollback note under Section 1: 'If the identity enum value is removed while identity tickets exist in the store, a compensating forward step is required — either retain the enum value as deprecated-but-recognised, or bulk-delete all identity tickets before removing the enum. Removing the enum value without cleanup causes reducer validation errors on existing identity events (no data loss, but read-path breakage).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define (or explicitly defer with a stated passthrough policy) the validation rule and error fallback for `keys[].algorithm` and `keys[].public_key` format at create time — either add a format note to the schema (e.g., 'stored as-is, validated by consumers') or specify a rejection error so the create path's error handling is unambiguous.
findings.finding: The `keys[].algorithm` vocabulary and `keys[].public_key` format are explicitly deferred to sibling ticket 4b5b ('The provider-vocabulary and key-format decisions are recorded as an ADR in sibling task 4b5b — this ticket adopts the defaults above'). No local schema validation rule, rejection condition, or error fallback for unrecognized/malformed `algorithm` or an ill-formed `public_key` string is defined in this ticket. A CREATE event carrying `algorithm: 'rsa-4096'` or `public_key: 'not-a-key'` would be silently accepted with no defined handling.
findings.location: §2 'Create path' — keys field definition; Acceptance Criteria AC2

findings.finding: The plan asserts `identity` can mirror the `session_log` exemption pattern, but the exemption is implemented as hardcoded literals at four distinct sites that the plan does not acknowledge: (1) `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` tuple in `reducer/_api.py:23`; (2) `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES` frozenset in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py:13`; (3) the `TicketType` Literal in the generated `types.py:22`; and (4) the `ticket_type` enum in `common.schema.json:13`. Omitting any one of these leaves a seam un-patched silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the plan's committed work: 'Update `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` in `reducer/_api.py`, `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES` in `_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py`, and the `ticket_type` enum in `common.schema.json` (then regenerate `types.py` via `python -m rebar.schemas.gen_types`). Add leak-loud tests asserting `identity` is absent from `_LOCAL_TO_JIRA_TYPE` and present in `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES`, mirroring `test_outbound_differ_session_log_exclusion.py`.'

findings.finding: The plan does not enumerate the existing code symbols/files that must be updated to register 'identity' as a new ticket type. At minimum, these consumers of the type registry exist and will require edits: `_TYPES` tuple in `src/rebar/_commands/composer.py`; `TicketType` Literal in `src/rebar/types.py`; `_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES` in `src/rebar/reducer/_api.py` (and its 5+ callers); `EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES` in `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py`; the exempt-type tuple in `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py` and `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/workflow_ops.py`. None of these are named in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Files to edit' section listing: src/rebar/_commands/composer.py (_TYPES, _FILE_IMPACT_EXEMPT_TYPES), src/rebar/types.py (TicketType Literal), src/rebar/reducer/_api.py (_NON_GRAPH_ARTIFACT_TYPES), src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/config.py (EXCLUDED_SYNC_TYPES), src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py and workflow_ops.py (exempt-type tuple).

findings.finding: AC3 describes 'a git-ignored config pointer records "which identity am I", defaulting to a git-email match' but does not specify: (a) the file path of the pointer (e.g. `.rebar/current_identity`), (b) its format (bare string, JSON object), or (c) which gitignore file is updated. The analogous session_log pattern names `.rebar/current_session_log` explicitly and is fully spec'd in the implementation.

The AC3 'defaulting to a git-email match' mechanism is incomplete: the plan specifies no behavior when the git email is unset, when zero identity tickets match it, or when multiple identity tickets share the same email — all realistic conditions in a fresh checkout or multi-contributor repo.
findings.location: AC3: 'A git-ignored config pointer records "which identity am I", defaulting to a git-email match.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a sentence to AC3 such as: 'If git email is unset or matches zero identity tickets, the pointer is left unset and callers receive None/a clear error; if multiple tickets match, the most-recently-created one wins (mirroring `start_session_log`'s last-writer-wins pointer).'

findings.finding: The git-email-match decision rule for AC3's config pointer has no specified fallback when git email is absent, unconfigured, or matches zero or multiple identity records.
findings.location: AC3: 'A git-ignored config pointer records "which identity am I", defaulting to a git-email match.'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a decision table to AC3: 'If git email resolves to exactly one identity → set pointer; if zero matches → pointer unset (nil identity, no error); if multiple matches → require explicit `rebar identity set <id>` and warn.'

findings.finding: AC3 describes a 'git-ignored config pointer' that records 'which identity am I' with a git-email-match default, but the plan body (Context section) contains no description of this mechanism — no file format, no location, no matching logic. AC3 is an orphan criterion: it covers work that is completely unspecified in the plan.
findings.suggested_fix: In the Context or a new 'Config pointer' subsection, specify: (1) the file path (e.g. `.rebar/identity` or `.rebar/me`, git-ignored via `.gitignore`), (2) its format (e.g. a single rebar identity id), (3) the default resolution: if the file is absent, scan identities for one whose email matches `git config user.email`, and error/warn if zero or multiple match.

findings.finding: AC3's observable outcome is not a specific system-state change but a vague feature description ('records which identity am I') with no artifact the reviewer can inspect to confirm completeness; it does not satisfy the 'specific observable outcome' bar.
AC3 has no proving command or test marker; there is no concrete, executable check that would confirm the git-ignored config pointer is correctly written, read, or falls back to git-email resolution.
The `{provider, external_id}` mappings field references an implicit `provider` enum whose value vocabulary is never defined in the plan or codebase.
findings.location: Context section: 'Holds name, email, `{provider,external_id}` mappings, and public keys'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a `## Schema` section to the plan listing allowed `provider` values as a named enum (e.g. `IDENTITY_PROVIDERS = {'github', 'jira', 'gerrit', 'claude'}`), reference it from the CREATE event schema, and specify whether unknown values are rejected at write time or stored with a warning.

findings.finding: The 'public keys' field has no format, algorithm, or encoding vocabulary defined — it is named but structurally unspecified.
findings.location: Context section: 'Holds name, email, `{provider,external_id}` mappings, and public keys'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
